description:
You are acting as the SRE on-call for a small Kubernetes-based web application.  
    Your goal is to perform a **service-level network audit** of the existing
    Kubernetes manifests that live in the directory  
    /home/user/manifests.  
    The audit must be written to the file  
    /home/user/network_diagnostics/service_audit.log (create the directory
    /home/user/network_diagnostics if it does not already exist).

    What you have:
    • Three YAML manifest files are already present under
      /home/user/manifests:  
          ‑ web-deployment.yaml  
          ‑ api-gateway.yaml  
          ‑ db.yaml

    • Each manifest can contain one or more Kubernetes objects (Service,
      Deployment, StatefulSet, …).

    What you must do (high level):
    1. Parse **every** Service object that appears anywhere inside those YAML
       files.  
    2. For each Service collect the following fields:
         • metadata.name  
         • spec.clusterIP  
         • spec.type   (if omitted, treat as “ClusterIP”)  
         • Every entry in spec.ports:
               – port  
               – targetPort  
               – protocol (default “TCP” if omitted)  
               – nodePort (may be absent)  
    3. Determine whether a Service has a NodePort that is **outside** the legal
       Kubernetes default range (30000–32767).  
         • If spec.type is *NodePort* **and** every nodePort value is inside the
           30000-32767 range → mark **OK**.  
         • If spec.type is *NodePort* **and** at least one nodePort value is
           < 30000 or > 32767 → mark **OUT_OF_RANGE**.  
         • If spec.type is anything else (ClusterIP, LoadBalancer, …) →
           mark **NOT_NODEPORT**.
    4. Write a strictly formatted log file summarising your findings.

    Required log-file format  
    (the automated checker compares this **exactly**, so follow precisely):

        # Service Audit Report
        Generated: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

        Service: <metadata.name>
        ClusterIP: <spec.clusterIP>
        Ports:
          - <port>/<protocol> -> <targetPort>
          - ...                             # one line per port
        Type: <spec.type|ClusterIP>
        NodePortIssues: <OK|OUT_OF_RANGE|NOT_NODEPORT>

        (blank line separates each service block)

        Summary:
        TotalServices: <integer>
        NodePortOutOfRange: <integer>

    Additional rules:
    • List the individual service blocks **in ascending alphabetical order of
      metadata.name**.  
    • Inside each service block list the Ports lines in the same order they
      appear in the manifest.  
    • The timestamp on the “Generated:” line must be the current UTC time in
      ISO-8601 format (use the literal letter “Z” for UTC).  
    • All indentation must be made with two spaces (no tabs).  
    • There must be exactly one blank line between consecutive service blocks
      and exactly one blank line between the last service block and the
      “Summary:” section.

    Verification hint:
    After you are done, you can `cat /home/user/network_diagnostics/service_audit.log`
    to visually inspect that the structure matches the specification.  The
    automated test will parse the file verbatim, so spacing, colons, and line
    breaks matter.

    Remember:
    • Do **not** attempt to apply or modify the manifests in a running cluster;
      this task is static analysis only.  
    • You do **not** have root privileges.  All work must happen inside the
      /home/user directory tree.  
    • Use only standard GNU/Linux command-line tools available in the default
      container (grep, awk, sed, cut, sort, etc.) or any scripting language
      that is pre-installed (bash, python3, …).  Do **not** install extra
      packages.

    When the log file exactly matches the required format and correct values,
    the task will be marked as complete.
truth:
The manifests that already exist before the agent starts:

    File: /home/user/manifests/web-deployment.yaml
    ------------------------------------------------
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Service
    metadata:
      name: web-service
    spec:
      selector:
        app: web
      type: NodePort
      ports:
        - port: 80
          targetPort: 8080
          protocol: TCP
          nodePort: 30080
      clusterIP: 10.96.0.10
    ---
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: web-deployment
    spec:
      replicas: 2
      selector:
        matchLabels:
          app: web
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: web
        spec:
          containers:
            - name: web
              image: nginx:1.19
              ports:
                - containerPort: 8080
              readinessProbe:
                httpGet:
                  path: /
                  port: 8080
              livenessProbe:
                httpGet:
                  path: /
                  port: 8080

    File: /home/user/manifests/api-gateway.yaml
    -------------------------------------------
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Service
    metadata:
      name: api-gateway
    spec:
      selector:
        app: gateway
      type: NodePort
      ports:
        - port: 443
          targetPort: 8443
          protocol: TCP
          nodePort: 40000
      clusterIP: 10.96.0.20
    ---
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: gateway-deployment
    spec:
      replicas: 1
      selector:
        matchLabels:
          app: gateway
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: gateway
        spec:
          containers:
            - name: gateway
              image: envoyproxy/envoy:v1.18.3
              ports:
                - containerPort: 8443
              readinessProbe:
                tcpSocket:
                  port: 8443
              livenessProbe:
                tcpSocket:
                  port: 8443

    File: /home/user/manifests/db.yaml
    ----------------------------------
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Service
    metadata:
      name: postgres
    spec:
      selector:
        app: postgres
      ports:
        - port: 5432
          protocol: TCP
      clusterIP: 10.96.0.30
    ---
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: StatefulSet
    metadata:
      name: postgres-stateful
    spec:
      serviceName: postgres
      replicas: 1
      selector:
        matchLabels:
          app: postgres
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: postgres
        spec:
          containers:
            - name: postgres
              image: postgres:13
              ports:
                - containerPort: 5432
              readinessProbe:
                tcpSocket:
                  port: 5432
              livenessProbe:
                tcpSocket:
                  port: 5432

    Expected values the checker will look for in
    /home/user/network_diagnostics/service_audit.log
    (timestamp will be ignored during comparison):

    # Service Audit Report
    Generated: <any-ISO8601-UTC-timestamp>

    Service: api-gateway
    ClusterIP: 10.96.0.20
    Ports:
      - 443/TCP -> 8443
    Type: NodePort
    NodePortIssues: OUT_OF_RANGE

    Service: postgres
    ClusterIP: 10.96.0.30
    Ports:
      - 5432/TCP -> 5432
    Type: ClusterIP
    NodePortIssues: NOT_NODEPORT

    Service: web-service
    ClusterIP: 10.96.0.10
    Ports:
      - 80/TCP -> 8080
    Type: NodePort
    NodePortIssues: OK

    Summary:
    TotalServices: 3
    NodePortOutOfRange: 1
